summary: Descriptive overview of duressdefense in substantive law: immediate threat, reasonableness, homicide exception.
key_points: immediate threat is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for duressdefense are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine duressdefense in litigation.
The doctrine of duressdefense has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding duressdefense requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
